question: What is Novo Nordisk's technical/options/quant setup?

findings.claim: Key support level at ~$36, which is the 52-week low area ($35.85 on Mar 3). Stock bounced from $35.85 and is currently testing this zone again at $36.84. A break below would be a new low with no nearby support.
findings.data: Bounce points computed from daily bars: 2026-03-03 low $35.85, 2026-03-13 low $37.74. Current price $36.84 is only $1 above the 52-week low. Recent hourly bars show the stock briefly dipped to $36.18 on 2026-03-20 before recovering to $36.84.

findings.claim: First resistance at ~$39 (recent rejection), then $43-44 (Feb bounce), then $48-50 (major consolidation zone where stock spent 35 days). The gap between current price and first meaningful resistance at $48 is massive (30%+ upside needed).
findings.data: Rejection points: 2026-03-09 high $39.88, 2026-03-17 high $39.26. Feb bounce low at $43.24 (2026-02-05). Price level frequency shows $48-50 zone had 45 combined days of trading, making it the strongest resistance cluster.

findings.claim: The stock has formed a staircase of lower highs and lower lows since mid-2024, with each bounce failing at a lower level. Recent bounces: $64 (Jan 23) -> $51 (Feb 9) -> $39 (Mar 9/17). This is textbook bearish structure.
findings.data: Sequential rejection points: 2026-01-23 high $64.16, 2026-02-09 high $51.08, 2026-02-17 high $49.84, 2026-03-09 high $39.88, 2026-03-17 high $39.26. Each rally fails at a lower level. Sequential bounce lows: 2025-12-09 low $46.09, 2026-02-05 low $43.24, 2026-03-03 low $35.85.
